Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Assisted Human Reproduction Act
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Assisted Human Reproduction Act

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Assisted Human Reproduction Act to decriminalize payment for sperm or ovum donation and for surrogacy in certain circumstances.
The enactment also amends the Act to allow for the purchase of other human reproductive material.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Assisted Human Reproduction Act is amended by adding âandâ at the end of paragraph (e) and by repealing paragraph (f).

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 1:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portions of section 2:

Section.Text: The Parliament of Canada recognizes and declares that

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: persons who seek to undergo assisted reproduction procedures must not be discriminated against, including on the basis of their sexual orientation or marital status;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: trade in the reproductive capabilities of women and men and the exploitation of children, women and men for commercial ends raise health and ethical concerns that justify their prohibition; and

Section.Text: Section 6 of the Act is replaced by the following:

No person shall counsel or induce a female person to become a surrogate mother, or perform any medical procedure to assist a female person to become a surrogate mother, knowing or having reason to believe that she is

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: incapable of consenting to becoming a surrogate mother; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: being coerced by a third party to become a surrogate mother.

Subsection.Text: No person shall pay consideration to a female person to be a surrogate mother, offer to pay such consideration or advertise that it will be paid.
No person shall accept consideration for arranging for the services of a surrogate mother, offer to make such an arrangement for consideration or advertise the arranging of such services.
No person shall pay consideration to another person to arrange for the services of a surrogate mother, offer to pay such consideration or advertise the payment of it.

No person shall counsel or induce a female person to become a surrogate mother, or perform any medical procedure to assist a female person to become a surrogate mother, knowing or having reason to believe that the female person is under 21 years of age.
This section does not affect the validity under provincial law of any agreement under which a person agrees to be a surrogate mother.
Subsection 7(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Sperm or ovum donation â requirements

Subsection.Text: No person shall, except for a purpose permitted under section 9, counsel or induce a person to donate their sperm or ova, or perform any medical procedure to assist a person to donate their sperm or ova, knowing or having reason to believe that the person is

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: incapable of consenting to the donation; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: being coerced by a third party to donate.

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 3:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portions of section 7:

Subsection.Text: No person shall purchase, offer to purchase or advertise for the purchase of sperm or ova from a donor or a person acting on behalf of a donor.

No person shall purchase, offer to purchase or advertise for the purchase of a human cell or gene from a donor or a person acting on behalf of a donor, with the intention of using the gene or cell to create a human being or of making it available for that purpose.
Subsection 7(3) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 12(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
No person shall, except in accordance with the regulations, reimburse any person for an expenditure incurred in the maintenance or transport of an in vitro embryo.
No person shall, except in accordance with the regulations,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: reimburse a donor for an expenditure incurred in the course of donating sperm or an ovum;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: reimburse any person for an expenditure incurred in the maintenance or transport of an in vitro embryo; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: reimburse a surrogate mother for an expenditure incurred by her in relation to her surrogacy.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 12(3) of the Act is repealed.

Section.Text: Paragraph 65(1)â(e.â1) of the Act is repealed.

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 5:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of relevant portions of subsection 65(1):

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations for carrying into effect the purposes and provisions of this Act and, in particular, may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsection 12(3), respecting the reimbursement of a loss of income;

Section.Text: This Act comes into force 180 days after the day on which it receives royal assent.
